NAME

io.c - IO capture implementation.

SYNOPSIS

Macros


#define LOG_IO_BUFFER_SIZE 8192

Functions


log_io_t log_io_start (void)
Start capturing stdout and stderr. void log_io_stop (log_io_t capture, const char *prefix)
Stop capturing and log the accumulated output.

Detailed Description

IO capture implementation.

Definition in file io.c.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define LOG_IO_BUFFER_SIZE 8192

Definition at line 13 of file io.c.

Referenced by log_io_stop().

Function Documentation

log_io_t log_io_start (void )

Start capturing stdout and stderr. Redirects both stdout and stderr to a pipe so their output can be captured and logged. Call log_io_stop() to end capture and log the accumulated output.

Returns

Capture handle (use with log_io_stop)

Note

If capture fails, returns a handle with saved_stdout_fd = -1

Do not use stdout/stderr directly while capturing

Definition at line 15 of file io.c.

15                             {
16   log_io_t capture = {.saved_stdout_fd = -1, .saved_stderr_fd = -1, .pipe_fd = -1};
17 
18   // Save original stdout and stderr
19   capture.saved_stdout_fd = dup(STDOUT_FILENO);
20   capture.saved_stderr_fd = dup(STDERR_FILENO);
21 
22   if (capture.saved_stdout_fd < 0 || capture.saved_stderr_fd < 0) {
23     if (capture.saved_stdout_fd >= 0)
24       close(capture.saved_stdout_fd);
25     if (capture.saved_stderr_fd >= 0)
26       close(capture.saved_stderr_fd);
27     return (log_io_t){.saved_stdout_fd = -1, .saved_stderr_fd = -1, .pipe_fd = -1};
28   }
29 
30   // Create a pipe for capturing output
31   int pipefd[2];
32   if (pipe(pipefd) < 0) {
33     close(capture.saved_stdout_fd);
34     close(capture.saved_stderr_fd);
35     return (log_io_t){.saved_stdout_fd = -1, .saved_stderr_fd = -1, .pipe_fd = -1};
36   }
37 
38   // pipefd[0] is read end, pipefd[1] is write end
39   int write_fd = pipefd[1];
40   capture.pipe_fd = pipefd[0];
41 
42   // Make the read end non-blocking so we can read without hanging
43   int flags = fcntl(capture.pipe_fd, F_GETFL, 0);
44   if (flags >= 0) {
45     fcntl(capture.pipe_fd, F_SETFL, flags | O_NONBLOCK);
46   }
47 
48   // Redirect both stdout and stderr to the pipe
49   if (dup2(write_fd, STDOUT_FILENO) < 0 || dup2(write_fd, STDERR_FILENO) < 0) {
50     close(write_fd);
51     close(capture.pipe_fd);
52     close(capture.saved_stdout_fd);
53     close(capture.saved_stderr_fd);
54     return (log_io_t){.saved_stdout_fd = -1, .saved_stderr_fd = -1, .pipe_fd = -1};
55   }
56 
57   // Close the write end in the parent process (we only need it in redirected streams)
58   close(write_fd);
59 
60   return capture;
61 }




References log_io_t::pipe_fd,
    log_io_t::saved_stderr_fd, and log_io_t::saved_stdout_fd.






void
  log_io_stop (log_io_t capture, const char * prefix)


Stop capturing and log the accumulated output. Restores stdout and
    stderr to their original state, reads all captured output from the pipe, and
    logs it at INFO level with the given prefix.




Parameters


capture Capture handle returned by
  log_io_start()


prefix Prefix to use in log messages (e.g., "ffmpeg",
  "portaudio")


Note


Safe to call with invalid handle (saved_stdout_fd = -1)



After calling this, the capture handle is invalidated




Definition at line 63 of file io.c.


63                                                        {
64   if (capture.saved_stdout_fd < 0 || capture.saved_stderr_fd < 0) {
65     return;  // Capture was not started successfully
66   }
67 
68   // Flush stdout/stderr BEFORE restoring to ensure all output is in the pipe
69   fflush(stdout);
70   fflush(stderr);
71 
72   // Restore stdout and stderr
73   dup2(capture.saved_stdout_fd, STDOUT_FILENO);
74   dup2(capture.saved_stderr_fd, STDERR_FILENO);
75   close(capture.saved_stdout_fd);
76   close(capture.saved_stderr_fd);
77 
78   // Read and log the captured output
79   if (capture.pipe_fd >= 0) {
80     char buffer[LOG_IO_BUFFER_SIZE];
81     ssize_t bytes_read;
82 
83     // Read all available data from the pipe
84     while ((bytes_read = read(capture.pipe_fd, buffer, sizeof(buffer) - 1)) > 0) {
85       buffer[bytes_read] = '\0';
86 
87       // Split by lines and log each line
88       char *line_start = buffer;
89       char *line_end;
90 
91       while ((line_end = strchr(line_start, '\n')) != NULL) {
92         // Null-terminate the line (overwriting the newline)
93         *line_end = '\0';
94 
95         // Log the line if it's not empty
96         if (line_start[0] != '\0') {
97           if (prefix) {
98             log_debug("[%s] %s", prefix, line_start);
99           } else {
100             log_debug("%s", line_start);
101           }
102         }
103 
104         // Move to the next line
105         line_start = line_end + 1;
106       }
107 
108       // Log any remaining partial line
109       if (line_start[0] != '\0') {
110         if (prefix) {
111           log_debug("[%s] %s", prefix, line_start);
112         } else {
113           log_debug("%s", line_start);
114         }
115       }
116     }
117 
118     close(capture.pipe_fd);
119   }
120 }






References log_debug, LOG_IO_BUFFER_SIZE,
    log_io_t::pipe_fd, log_io_t::saved_stderr_fd, and
    log_io_t::saved_stdout_fd.
